user_title: Head of Change Execution, WPB Private Banking & Wealth Solutions, Asia Pacific

talking_points.headline: HSBC Wealth Powerhouse Goes Live: $1.4T AUM, Half from Asia — APAC Change Execution Bears the Growth Thesis
talking_points.why_it_matters: The WPB consolidation into a global wealth powerhouse crystallises the delivery mandate for Adam's APAC change programme — RM hiring, new wealth centres, and product expansion across Asia are now time-bound strategic commitments that require accelerated execution.
bullets: HSBC's WPB combines retail banking, wealth management, and global private banking into one of the world's largest wealth managers — US$1.4 trillion in assets, nearly half from Asia, targeting high-single-digit to 10% AUM CAGR over five years
Accelerated RM hiring, new wealth centres, and expanded products are active across HK, Singapore, India, and Mainland China — each strand requires dedicated change execution capacity across APAC

Amy Lo, Asia Wealth Chair at UBS Global Wealth Management, confirmed May 28 that UBS continues to hire across HK and Asia; Deutsche Bank plans a 50% EM headcount increase — HSBC's delivery velocity must lead, not follow

source_links.title: HSBC: Global Wealth Hub in Asia Push
UBS Asia Chair: Continues to Hire in Region

talking_points.headline: BCG Prices the Agentic AI Mandate: 30% Profit Uplift, 40% Cost Cut by 2030 — Wealth RM Copilots the Priority Workflow
talking_points.why_it_matters: BCG's 2026 analysis gives Adam a Board-ready ROI case for accelerating HSBC WPB's AI delivery — agentic advisor copilots and compliance automation are the highest-value change execution targets in H2 2026.
bullets: BCG finds agentic AI can increase retail bank profitability by 30% and reduce costs up to 40% by 2030 — agents represent 17% of AI-derived banking value today, rising to 29% by 2028

In wealth management, agentic transformation means RM copilots surfacing next-best actions, preparing portfolios, and compressing compliance administration — Lloyds Banking Group has agents live across customer journeys, declaring 2026 'the year of agentic AI'
HSBC WPB must embed agentic tooling into the APAC change delivery model in H2 2026 — DBS (430 use cases) and StanChart (AI Factory) are widening the operational AI gap with every quarter of delay

source_links.title: BCG: How Retail Banks Can Put AI Agents to Work
Lloyds: 2026 — The Year of Agentic AI

talking_points.headline: DBS Completes Euromoney Double — World's Best Plus Asia's Best — Making the HSBC Delivery Gap Visible
talking_points.why_it_matters: DBS's second Euromoney award in a week cements its status as the benchmark Adam's APAC AI-enabled change programme must close — Euromoney methodology directly rewards AI deployment, platform innovation, and AUM growth velocity.
bullets: Euromoney named DBS Asia's Best Private Bank 2026, following last week's World's Best win — DBS doubled AUM in five years, delivered record Q1 2026 wealth income of SGD 2.93bn, and is distributing private credit yielding up to 20% through its platform

Standard Chartered is simultaneously strengthening advisory credentials by hiring senior CIO strategists from UBS GWM and JPMorgan Asset Management — competitive pressure on HSBC WPB's differentiators is now multi-directional

HSBC WPB's APAC change programme must prioritise measurable AI and platform milestones that can be evidenced externally — the next award cycle will determine whether HSBC or competitors own the WPB momentum narrative heading into 2027

source_links.title: Euromoney: DBS Asia's Best Private Bank 2026
DBS/Granite Asia: 20% Yield Private Credit Fund
StanChart Hires UBS GWM and JPMAM Strategists

AI & Technology.title: How Retail Banks Can Put Agentic AI to Work
AI & Technology.summary: BCG finds agentic AI can increase retail bank profitability by 30% and cut costs up to 40% by 2030; agents already represent 17% of AI-derived banking value, rising to 29% by 2028, with wealth management RM productivity as the highest-ROI deployment use case.

AI & Technology.title: AI in Private Wealth Management: From Experimentation to Agentic Transformation
AI & Technology.summary: Private wealth firms are transitioning from AI pilots to full agentic transformation, with an AI orchestration layer coordinating banking systems to free RMs for strategic client conversations — only 35% of advisors use AI tools daily today.

AI & Technology.title: BCG Global Asset Management Report 2026: Rebuilding for an AI-First World
AI & Technology.summary: BCG's 2026 flagship report calls for rebuilding institutions around an AI-first model — autonomous agentic AI supervising end-to-end workflows across service, compliance, risk, and exceptions at near-zero marginal cost, unlocking a $370bn annual retail banking profit pool by 2030.

HSBC News.title: HSBC in Powerhouse Mode: Global Wealth Hub in Asia Push
HSBC News.summary: HSBC's WPB combines retail banking, wealth management, and global private banking into one of the world's largest wealth managers — US$1.4 trillion in assets, nearly half from Asia — with accelerated RM hiring, new wealth centres, and product expansion targeting HK, Singapore, India, and Mainland China.

HSBC News.title: HSBC Swiss Private Bank Appoints Alfonso Gómez as CEO
HSBC News.summary: HSBC Swiss Private Bank appointed Alfonso Gómez as CEO effective April 27 — joining from BBVA Switzerland where he served as CEO for over 12 years, adding senior European private banking leadership to HSBC's ongoing global management realignment.

Competitor Intelligence.title: UBS Asia Wealth Chair: Bank Continues to Hire Across the Region
Competitor Intelligence.summary: Amy Lo, UBS Asia Wealth Chair, confirmed at the May 28 UBS Asian Investment Conference that UBS continues to hire across Hong Kong and Asia in 2026 — the bank manages USD 6.9 trillion in invested assets and is integrating a new $1.8T unified public-and-private-markets platform.

Competitor Intelligence.title: UBS Asset Management Unifies Public and Private Markets Under $1.8 Trillion Platform
Competitor Intelligence.summary: UBS Asset Management is consolidating public and private market capabilities under a single $1.8 trillion platform — creating integrated investment solutions that compete directly with HSBC WPB's multi-asset advisory offering across Asia's UHNW and family office segments.

Competitor Intelligence.title: Standard Chartered Hires Former UBS GWM and JPMAM Strategists
Competitor Intelligence.summary: Standard Chartered hired a UBS Global Wealth Management CIO equity strategist (responsible for the global AI portfolio) and Jonathan Liang from JPMorgan Asset Management as CIO for fixed income and FX — reinforcing advisory credentials as it pursues $200bn in net new money by 2028.

Private Banking & Wealth.title: Asia's Wealth Shift: Hong Kong and Singapore Lead the Next Chapter in Family Offices
Private Banking & Wealth.summary: Citywealth reports family offices in Hong Kong and Singapore have quadrupled since 2020, with sophisticated families increasingly running dual HK-and-SG structures to optimise governance, tax efficiency, and investment footprint — further acceleration projected through 2028.

Private Banking & Wealth.title: UBS: Asia-Pacific Heirs Turn to Wealth Professionals for Succession Advice
Private Banking & Wealth.summary: UBS's inaugural Global Next Generation Report finds 40%+ of Asia-Pacific families are in active wealth transfer or succession planning — 72% of heirs seek professional guidance first, outpacing European and North American peers and opening a structural advisory demand window for private banks.

Private Banking & Wealth.title: DBS/Granite Asia Private Credit Fund Yields Up to 20% for Wealthy Clients
Private Banking & Wealth.summary: Granite Asia partnered with DBS Private Bank to distribute a private credit fund targeting yields up to 20% to HNW clients — demonstrating DBS's capability to deliver institutional-grade alternative products through its private banking platform.

Regulatory & Markets.title: Hong Kong's Family Office 2.0: Tax Modernisation Meets Geopolitical Risk Rebalancing
Regulatory & Markets.summary: Hong Kong's Family Office 2.0 initiative (2026–2028) extends tax exemptions to gold, digital assets, private credit, and overseas property — combined with Middle East tensions driving family offices to Asia, this creates a H2 2026 HNWI onboarding inflection point for HSBC WPB.

Regulatory & Markets.title: EY Global Financial Services Regulatory Outlook 2026: 4 Shifts Firms Must Watch
Regulatory & Markets.summary: EY's 2026 Global Regulatory Outlook identifies four key shifts: AI governance frameworks, operational resilience mandates, sustainable finance taxonomy convergence, and intensified third-party risk oversight — all directly relevant to HSBC WPS's H2 2026 change and compliance priorities.

Operations & Change.title: US Banks Big on Asia Hiring in 2026: India GCCs Lead, HK and Singapore Active
Operations & Change.summary: US banks are intensifying Asia hiring in 2026 — India's Global Capability Centres have hundreds of openings in technology and operations, while HK and Singapore offices are adding tens of senior roles — deepening the talent market competition HSBC WPB faces across its APAC delivery footprint.
